playlists.title: Ethics & Soft Skills Interview
playlists.tagline: The questions that decide offers — with frameworks, not scripts
playlists.description: Deep coaching on behavioral rounds (VERVE-style) and ethics probes at mission-driven companies. Each episode: what they're testing, sample questions, strong vs weak answers, and a live mock.

episodes.title: Ownership & big picture
episodes.problemYouSolve: Prove you own outcomes — not tickets — and connect work to impact.
sampleQuestions: Tell me about a time you took ownership of a project that was failing or at risk.
What was the broader context, what did you do, and what was the outcome?
What would you have done if you had half the time?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: STAR in 90 seconds: Situation (stakes + stakeholders) → your specific actions (I, not we) → measurable result → one thing you'd do differently.
strongSignals: Names stakeholders, constraints, and timeline pressure
Quantifies outcome or cites measurable improvement
Acknowledges what they would do differently
weakPatterns: Vague team credit without personal contribution
Purely technical answer with no product or user lens
episodes.coachTip: Interviewers listen for judgment under ambiguity. The real problem is often organizational, not technical.

episodes.title: Pushback on No
episodes.problemYouSolve: Show you advocate with evidence — then commit fully after a decision.
sampleQuestions: Describe a situation where leadership said no to something you believed was important.
How did you respond?
What evidence would have changed the outcome?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Sequence: understand the no → present data or user evidence (not opinion) → propose phased/reversible alternative → accept decision → execute with full commitment.
strongSignals: Presents data or user evidence, not just opinion
Accepts final decision and executes fully
Maintains relationship with the decider
weakPatterns: Framing the story as winning an argument
episodes.coachTip: Senior signal: knowing when to stop advocating and when to escalate with new information.

episodes.title: Force multiplier & mentorship
episodes.problemYouSolve: Demonstrate you make the team faster — not just yourself.
sampleQuestions: Tell me about a time you helped someone on your team level up.
How did you decide what to teach vs delegate?
What artifacts did you leave behind?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Before/after team capability: specific mentoring actions (pairing, docs, reviews) → mentee or team outcome → what you learned from them.
strongSignals: Specific mentoring actions (pairing, docs, runbooks)
Junior or peer grew measurable skill or autonomy
Balanced helping with personal deadlines
weakPatterns: Only story is doing everyone's work yourself
No outcome for the mentee or team
episodes.coachTip: They want systems thinking: reduced bus factor, not heroics.

episodes.title: Pragmatism vs perfectionism
episodes.problemYouSolve: Show you ship with explicit trade-offs — and protect what must not break.
sampleQuestions: Give an example where you deliberately shipped something imperfect.
What did you cut and what did you protect?
What would you not cut even under pressure?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: MVP scope vs v2 plan: name what was deferred and why it was safe → flags/monitoring post-ship → stakeholder alignment on scope.
strongSignals: Names what was deferred and why it was safe
Monitors post-ship and iterates
Aligns with PM/stakeholders on scope
weakPatterns: Perfectionism — endless refactor with no ship
Reckless shortcuts in security or data integrity
Cannot articulate what good enough meant
episodes.coachTip: The trap is sounding reckless OR sounding like you never ship. Name the quality bar explicitly.

episodes.title: Your questions for them
episodes.problemYouSolve: Turn the end of the interview into proof you're evaluating them too.
sampleQuestions: We're at the end — what questions do you have for me?
What does success look like at 30 and 90 days?
How do code review and on-call work on this team?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Prepare 5 questions across: team dynamics, product trade-offs, definition of done, growth, and risks. Build on their answers — show active listening.
strongSignals: Asks about on-call, code review culture, or definition of done
Connects questions to their past experience
Follows up on interviewer answers thoughtfully
episodes.coachTip: Senior candidates ask about architecture risks and how decisions get made — not just perks.

episodes.title: Ethics & dual-use (frontier labs)
episodes.problemYouSolve: Answer ethics probes at DeepMind, Anthropic, and mission orgs without sounding naive or evasive.
sampleQuestions: How would you handle a feature that could be misused if deployed broadly?
Tell me about a time you raised an ethical concern on a technical decision.
Where do you draw the line between moving fast and responsible deployment?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Name stakeholders affected → concrete harm scenario → mitigation (gates, monitoring, rollback) → who you escalated to → outcome. Avoid both 'I'd refuse everything' and 'ship and pray'.
strongSignals: Specific harm scenario, not abstract ethics
Proposes technical and process mitigations
Escalated through proper channels with documentation
weakPatterns: No example from real work or credible hypothetical
episodes.coachTip: Frontier interviews probe whether you'll flag risks before they're external. Show you think in blast radius.

episodes.title: Team fit under pressure
episodes.problemYouSolve: Show you disagree well, recover from conflict, and keep shipping as a teammate.
sampleQuestions: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate on a technical decision.
Describe a conflict that didn't resolve cleanly — what happened?
How do you give hard feedback to someone more senior than you?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Conflict → your role → how you separated ego from idea → resolution or learned compromise → relationship after. Never make the other person the villain.
strongSignals: Acknowledges valid points on both sides
Describes specific communication tactics
Team still shipped; relationship intact
weakPatterns: Conflict avoided at cost of product quality
episodes.coachTip: Team fit is not 'everyone likes me' — it's 'I make hard calls without breaking trust'.
